_provenance.note: A FOURTH literature (Epic U): martingale concentration inequalities (McDiarmid / Bernstein), lifted into grounded dgroups with the same higher-order CAUSE(premise -> guarantee) schema as the conformal, optimization, and learning corpora. Used to stress the unsupervised role-discovery and cross-domain analogy at greater scale: if the 'bounded-difference martingale' relation is discovered to play the SAME structural-property role (PC/2) as weighted-exchangeability / contraction / uniform-convergence with no new design, the analogy web extends to four fields automatically. Grounded; every symbol is a verbatim substring of its statement, checked by grounding.verify.

mcdiarmid_concentration.name: mcdiarmid_concentration
mcdiarmid_concentration.text: A function with bounded differences in each coordinate forms a bounded-difference martingale over the independent inputs, and that bounded-difference martingale drives a concentration inequality for the function around its mean deviation.

bernstein_concentration.name: bernstein_concentration
bernstein_concentration.text: Under a variance bound on the increments, a function with bounded differences in each coordinate forms a bounded-difference martingale over the independent inputs; the variance bound sharpens the bounded-difference martingale, which drives a concentration inequality for the function around its mean deviation.
